title: Lazy-Bastard Score Computation and Re-Review Flag

story.as_a: tenant operator relying on soft signals to catch agents declaring work done without actually doing it
story.i_want_to: have loopctl compute a lazy-bastard score for every completed story based on telemetry anomalies relative to the story's complexity, and auto-flag high-scoring stories for a more scrupulous re-review
story.so_that: prematurely-declared stories get caught by additional scrutiny even when the code technically compiles and tests pass

rationale: With telemetry from US-26.6.1 flowing, scoring it mechanically catches the most common lazy patterns: too-few tool calls, too-short CoT, mismatch between tests_run and test ACs. The score is never a hard block — it triggers re-review, which the chain-of-custody system handles normally.

acceptance_criteria.description: `Loopctl.Stories.compute_lazy_score/1` takes a story and returns a score 0-100 based on these weighted signals: (a) tool_call_count vs baseline for stories of similar estimated_hours (weight 25), (b) cot_length_tokens vs baseline (weight 15), (c) tests_run_count vs count of ACs with type:test criterion (weight 30), (d) time_to_completion vs estimated_hours (weight 15), (e) ratio of commits to files_modified (weight 15). Each subscore 0-100.
Baseline is computed per tenant from the last 30 days of successfully verified stories. Fallback to a loopctl-global baseline if the tenant has fewer than 10 verified stories.
Score is computed at `request_review` time and stored on the story as `lazy_score` (integer) and `lazy_score_breakdown` (jsonb with per-signal subscores).

Stories with `lazy_score >= 75` are flagged `re_review_required: true`. The rotating verifier selection for re-review stories uses a more-scrupulous verifier prompt (system article `re-review-prompt` describes the extra checks).
Lazy scores and flags are visible in the admin story dashboard. Operator can manually flag or unflag a story with a note; both actions write audit_chain entries.
The thresholds (75 for flag, weights per signal) are tunable per tenant via tenant settings. Default values ship in config.
Documented: the scoring is a soft signal — the story still progresses through normal verification even at score 100. Flags change WHO reviews, not WHETHER the story can pass.

System article `lazy-bastard-detection` (from US-26.4.5) updated with the scoring formula and thresholds (in a way that doesn't give agents a gaming recipe — documents what's measured but not the exact weights).
Migration adds `stories.lazy_score` and `stories.lazy_score_breakdown` columns. CHANGELOG entry.

test_cases.name: Low tool_call_count drives up the score
preconditions: Baseline of 50 tool calls for 4-hour stories
steps: Create story with 4h estimate and 5 tool calls
expected_results: Score is high (> 50) due to the tool call subscore

test_cases.name: Normal telemetry yields a low score
steps: Story matching baseline on all signals

test_cases.name: High score sets re_review_required flag
steps: Submit a story with deliberately low telemetry

technical_notes: Baseline queries use AdminRepo to read cross-tenant or loopctl-global data.
Subscore formulas are documented in an internal module module-docstring for future tuning.
The loopctl-global baseline serves new tenants; fallback triggers when tenant has < 10 verified stories.
